Title: A comparative transcriptomics and eQTL approach identifies SlWD40 as a tomato fruit ripening regulator

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit nutritional and quality traits including red pigmentation and umami flavor
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: fruit ripening-associated carotenoid biosynthesis, ethylene biosynthesis/signaling, cell wall metabolism, sugar and amino acid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive regulation, likely via interaction/co-action with ripening TF network
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ACS4; PSY; Z-ISO; PL; PMEI; INV; BCAT2; THA1; RIN; NOR; AP2a-associated network
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Overexpression upregulated ripening-related genes including RIN, NOR, PSY, PL and promoted ACS4, Z-ISO, INV, BCAT2, THA1 expression; RNAi downregulated these genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ripening stage; ethylene-related inputs; RIN direct binding to SlWD40 promoter; promoter contains AP2, B3, EIN3/EIL, C2H2, MADS, NF-YB/A/C cis-elements
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes tomato fruit ripening; overexpression accelerates chlorophyll degradation and lycopene accumulation, whereas RNAi delays ripening and reduces lycopene and glutamate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: important positive ripening regulator acting in concert with major regulators RIN and NOR
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Proposed facilitator/scaffold with co-expressed TFs; discussion notes WD40 proteins can act in MYB-bHLH-WD40 complexes, but specific SlWD40 ripening complex not experimentally proven here
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RIN; NOR; AP2a; SlWRKY17; SlWRKY75; SlWRKY37; SlWRKY23; SlWRKY30; SlWRKY6; SlWRKY31; SlWRKY79
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low in leaf, bud, flower, root, and young fruit; strongly induced after mature green stage during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In fruit, expression increases exponentially after mature green stage; high in ripening fruit cell types similar to RIN and NOR
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental stages MG, breaker, pink; VIGS in MicroTom Del/Ros1 fruit
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi fruits were smaller and showed delayed ripening; broader shifts in amino acids, lipids, and secondary metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative transcriptomics; eQTL mapping; VIGS; stable fruit-specific overexpression; RNAi knockdown; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; co-expression network analysis; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; transcriptomics; metabolomics. Clean RNA-seq data available in Zenodo: 10.5281/zenodo.5525948; 10.5281/zenodo.5525946
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to accelerate or modulate tomato ripening and improve carotenoid/flavor-associated quality traits using fruit-specific expression or editing; should consider fruit size and broad metabolome effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified through comparative transcriptomics plus eQTL; defined as cis-eQTL in IL4-1 and IL4-1-1; VIGS caused yellowish phenotype; transcriptome patterns in RNAi resembled rin and nor mutants
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlWD40 was identified by comparative transcriptomics and eQTL mapping as a candidate ripening regulator and validated by VIGS. 2. Fruit-specific overexpression accelerated ripening, chlorophyll breakdown, and lycopene accumulation, whereas RNAi delayed ripening and reduced carotenoid accumulation. 3. SlWD40 positively affected expression of key ripening/metabolic genes including ACS4, PSY, Z-ISO, PL, INV, BCAT2, and THA1, and RNAi metabolomes showed reduced glutamate and altered lipids/secondary metabolites similar in part to rin and nor.
